dailyNews.title: Schools, water, industry: What civilian targets have US, Israel, Iran hit?
dailyNews.description: US and Israel have increasingly been hitting civilian sites in Iran as efforts to end the weeks-long war are under way.

dailyNews.title: Russian tanker reaches Cuba months after Donald Trump cut fuel supplies; has US stance changed?
dailyNews.description: Cuba faces severe power blackouts, affecting hospitals, as the US oil blockade hampers energy supplies. A Russian tanker delivered crude oil, seen as vital for the nation amid the crisis. Trump's softened stance may ease restrictions, but Cuba's energy challenges remain significant.

dailyNews.title: Palestine aIly Ireland under fire for allowing weapons transfers to Israel
dailyNews.description: Since the Iraq War, Shannon Airport has served as a key transit hub for the US military. It is now under scrutiny again.

dailyNews.title: Ex-CIA station chief warns US troop deployment to key Iranian island could be 'extremely risky'
dailyNews.description: Former CIA station chief Dan Hoffman warns sending U.S. troops to Iran's Kharg Island is "extremely risky" due to ballistic missile and drone threats.

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court declines to hear 'Tiger King' Joe Exotic's challenge after murder-for-hire conviction
Amanda Peet exposes ‘desperation galore’ behind Hollywood fame
dailyNews.description: Amanda Peet says Hollywood is "smoke and mirrors," speaking candidly about the competitive industry, aging and her breast cancer diagnosis.

dailyNews.title: NJ school district’s secretive transgender policy faces legal threat for bucking Supreme Court ruling
dailyNews.description: Thomas More Society threatens to sue a New Jersey school district over a policy allegedly hiding students' gender identity from parents, marking the first test of a recent Supreme Court ruling.

dailyNews.title: Karoline Leavitt blasts NYT ballroom coverage, calls out critics who 'never built anything'
dailyNews.description: Karoline Leavitt blasted The New York Times over an article about the White House ballroom plans, saying the project costs taxpayers nothing and is long overdue.

dailyNews.title: 49ers GM quells buzz around electrical substation theory
dailyNews.description: San Francisco 49ers general manager John Lynch shut down the buzz around the theory that an electrical substation was the catalyst for injuries on the team.

dailyNews.title: Chicago’s empty 'ghost schools' teach us about how the system is failing
dailyNews.description: Frederick Douglass Academy High School spends $93,000 per student with only 27 enrolled and zero students proficient in math or reading, data shows.

dailyNews.title: Pirates broadcasters slam Oneil Cruz's defensive effort vs Mets
dailyNews.description: Pittsburgh Pirates outfielder Oneil Cruz faced criticism from broadcasters on Sunday as he glided to a fly ball against the New York Mets on Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Gavin Newsom bashes Elon Musk as ‘one of the great disappointments'
dailyNews.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom told Axios that Elon Musk has “put the brakes" on EV innovation and warned China is overtaking the United States in the electric vehicle market.

dailyNews.title: Mark Cuban credits his 'disgusting' sandwich for helping him boost protein and beat aging
dailyNews.description: Entrepreneur Mark Cuban admits his go-to lunch of canned baby clams, egg white wraps, and honey is "disgusting" but says it fuels his protein and longevity goals.

dailyNews.title: Gavin Rossdale says co-parenting with Gwen Stefani is like a ‘two-lane highway' that never merges
dailyNews.description: Gavin Rossdale says co-parenting with Gwen Stefani is like a two-lane highway that never merges, but insists the system works for their kids.

dailyNews.description: Before President Trump’s order to limit birthright citizenship, there was widespread agreement that the 14th Amendment guaranteed citizenship for U.S.-born babies.

dailyNews.title: They’ve Been Accused of Running a ‘Covert’ Operation in Greenland. It’s No Secret.
dailyNews.description: Members of President Trump’s circle, working in plain sight, have caught the eye of Denmark’s intelligence services for trying to make friends and cut deals on the Danish territory.

dailyNews.title: President Trump claims 'regime change' has happened in Iran
dailyNews.description: President Trump spoke to reporters on Air Force One and claimed that "regime change" has happened in Iran since the conflict began and that it was imperative.

dailyNews.title: More Troops Arrive in Middle East as Trump Hails Iran Negotiations
dailyNews.description: As thousands more American troops arrive in the Middle East to join a widening war, President Donald Trump says Iran is softening its weekslong blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, after it allowed 20 boats through the oil route. It comes as the Washington Post reports that the Pentagon is preparing for weeks of ground operations in Iran, short of a full-scale invasion. NBC’s Matt Bradley reports for TODAY.

dailyNews.title: TSA Pay to Resume As Airport Security Bottleneck Persists
dailyNews.description: Travelers across the country are still dealing with delays at airport security days after President Donald Trump signed an executive order approving TSA officers’ back pay. The Department of Homeland Security says the officers could start getting paychecks as soon as Monday or Tuesday. NBC’s Priscilla Thompson reports for TODAY.

dailyNews.description: In an interview with NBC News’ Raf Sanchez, President Zelenskyy says that he hopes President Trump cares about the future of Ukraine, but “they care about the United States more.”

dailyNews.title: White House holds press briefing

dailyNews.title: Trump says U.S. will destroy Iran’s oil wells, Kharg Island without deal to 'immediately' reopen Hormuz Strait
dailyNews.description: Donald Trump warned the U.S. will completely obliterate all of Iran's electric generating plants, oil wells and Kharg Island if a peace deal is not reached.
